operating_state: Total revenue increased 4% to $124.3 billion driven by Services growth of 14% and Mac growth of 16%, while iPhone remained flat year-over-year. Regional performance showed broad strength with double-digit growth in Europe and Japan, offset by Greater China declining. Services expansion was led by advertising, App Store, and cloud services, while Mac growth reflected higher laptop sales.
cost_structure: Total gross margin expanded to 46.9% from 45.9% as Services gross margin increased to 75.0% from 72.8% due to favorable mix and higher volumes, while Products gross margin remained stable at 39.3%. Operating expenses increased to 12% of revenue driven by higher research and development costs from headcount expansion and infrastructure investments.
material_events: Kevan Parekh was appointed Chief Financial Officer effective January 1, 2025, succeeding Luca Maestri. The company paid €11.0 billion to Ireland in connection with the State Aid Decision with €3.3 billion remaining in escrow. Capital returns totaled $27.2 billion including $23.3 billion in share repurchases and $3.9 billion in dividends.
macro_exposures: Currency fluctuations had mixed regional impacts with unfavorable effects in Americas and favorable effects in Europe and Greater China. The company faces intensified regulatory scrutiny including antitrust investigations in multiple jurisdictions and potential impacts from DOJ remedies against Google that could affect search licensing revenue arrangements.
forward_outlook: The company maintains its quarterly dividend at $0.25 per share with intent to increase annually subject to board approval. Management expects continued investment in research and development and infrastructure to support product development. Current cash balances and operational cash flow will satisfy requirements and capital return programs over the next 12 months.
uncertainty_profile: Pending antitrust litigation in the U.S. alleging smartphone market monopolization could result in significant fines and business practice changes. European Commission challenges to Digital Markets Act compliance plans create ongoing regulatory uncertainty. New accounting standards will require enhanced disclosures beginning in fiscal 2025 and 2026.
